paraphrase: Northern lords secretly resent the Boltons and Freys over the Red Wedding; Wyman Manderly is confirmed to be plotting against the Boltons, and the Umbers and Dustins are suspected of the same.
prior_failed_quote: "The north remembers, Lord Davos. The north remembers, and the mummer's farce is almost done. My son is home." Something about the way Lord Wyman said that chilled Davos to the bone.
prior_note: Of the two ambiguous hits, Davos IV:125 (Manderly's monologue about vengeance) is the better match for the paraphrase about Northern lords secretly plotting against the Boltons, as it explicitly details Manderly's conspiracy against the Freys and reflects the theme of secret resentment.

paraphrase: Counter-evidence: Robett Glover knows that Bran and Rickon are actually alive, so it doesn't make sense for him to call Theon 'Kinslayer' for supposedly killing them.
prior_failed_quote: The north remembers, Lord Davos. The north remembers, and the mummer's farce is almost done. My son is home." Something about the way Lord Wyman said that chilled Davos to the bone. "If it is justice that you want, my lord, look to King Stannis. No man is more just." Robett Glover broke in to add, "Your loyalty does you honor, my lord, but Stannis Baratheon remains your king, not our own."
prior_note: Robett is present and aware of Manderly's anti-Bolton conspiracy with Stannis, so he would know Bran and Rickon are alive per the conspiracy knowledge-base.

paraphrase: Candidate: Harwin, one of Ned Stark's household guards, who helped teach Arya, Jon, and Robb to ride -- a trusted, loyal Stark servant.
prior_failed_quote: "Take the boys," Ned said to Harwin as the morning bells began to ring in the Red Keep, signaling the change of watch.
prior_note: Harwin established as Ned's trusted household guard and teacher to Stark children in early books.

paraphrase: In Book 1, Harwin was sent on a mission with Beric Dondarrion and Thoros of Myr, was attacked, and the group became the Brotherhood Without Banners; Harwin later has an emotional reunion with Arya when she joins the Brotherhood.
prior_failed_quote: Behind him, Arya recognised the brothers' sworn swords. Harwin had gone with them, she remembered. Harwin's broad face as he fell into the river...
prior_note: Establishes Harwin was sent with Beric Dondarrion and the group that became the Brotherhood, and his later reunification with Arya.

paraphrase: By Book 4, Harwin is with the Brotherhood group led by Lady Stoneheart -- the resurrected Catelyn Stark -- who is searching for her daughter Arya and questioning captives about her, and would by now have heard that 'Arya' has married Ramsay Bolton.
prior_failed_quote: "Did the man give a name?" asked Sam. "Harwin. A northman, the brothers said, one of the old Stark men-at-arms. He was searching for his daughter. She was missing, he believed, carried off by a brigand."
prior_note: Harwin appears with Lady Stoneheart's Brotherhood group searching for Arya (whom they believe is Lord Eddard's missing daughter).

paraphrase: When Theon accuses the wildling women of the murders, Rowan denies killing Little Walder but effectively admits that they killed Yellow Dick and the others.
prior_failed_quote: "That was not us. I told you." ... "You killed the others, why not him? Yellow Dick—" "—stank as bad as you. A pig of a man." ... "Not us." Rowan grabbed him by the throat and shoved him back against the barracks wall, her face an inch from his.
prior_note: Rowan denies Little Walder (line 111, 119) but admits Yellow Dick through contextual denial; effectively confesses to murdering Yellow Dick and others but not Little Walder

paraphrase: This mirrors Bran Stark's story: Bran falls from a tower, then dreams of flying and is visited in the dream by the three-eyed crow (Bloodraven), who opens his third eye to magic; Bran's maester likewise says he can't do magic, and is wrong.
prior_failed_quote: "Bran was falling, falling, falling off the castle, and when he woke in his bed he found he could not move his legs anymore."
prior_note: Parallel established: Bran tower fall parallels Euron's claimed flying dream; Bran visited by three-eyed crow (Bloodraven); the text implies maester was wrong about physical limitation

paraphrase: Some ironborn are said to be able to skinchange into sea creatures, showing the ability isn't exclusive to Starks/wargs -- supporting the idea that Euron could be a skinchanger too.
prior_failed_quote: "Some men can skin-change, most like, though they'll never speak of it. It's a grim magic."
prior_note: Wargs/skinchangers confirmed to exist among ironborn/northmen; establishes capacity for non-Stark individuals to possess the gift

paraphrase: In Bran's flying dream he sees the whole world spread out below him -- Winterfell, Asshai, dragons, and the heart of winter -- and it's theorized Euron may have seen the same things in his own dream, explaining his hunger for total power.
prior_failed_quote: "And suddenly Bran was soaring. The world spread below him like a map."
prior_note: Bran's dream shows 'the whole world' from above including Asshai and dragons; video speculation that Euron saw same in childhood dream

paraphrase: The Citadel in Oldtown holds magical Valyrian artefacts called glass candles.
prior_failed_quote: What feeds a dragon's fire? All Valyrian sorcery was rooted in blood or fire. The sorcerers of the Freehold could see across mountains, seas, and deserts with one of these glass candles.
prior_note: Confirms glass candles as magical Valyrian artifacts housed in Citadel

paraphrase: Thematic argument: Euron embodies 'silence', the opposite of the series' title A Song of Ice and Fire -- his ship is named Silence, he mutes his crew, and he silences his brothers through trauma; a sudden silence falls when he first enters the story.
prior_failed_quote: I swore to give you Westeros, and here is your first taste. A morsel, nothing more . . . but we shall feast before the fall of night!
prior_note: Euron's promises of feasting and conquest; Silence ship = opposite of Song; his power based on silence and fear

paraphrase: Ramsay is called a murderous 'beast in human skin' who hunts and rapes women for sport.
prior_failed_quote: The Boltons have always been as cruel as they were cunning, but this one seems a beast in human skin.

paraphrase: Robett Glover is present at the meeting with Davos and may be in contact with his brother Galbart; there are hints Robett may raise an army.
prior_failed_quote: Robett Glover took up the tale. We may never know all that happened at Winterfell...

paraphrase: Catelyn Stark, another witness to the will, is resurrected by Beric Dondarrion as Lady Stoneheart, who leads the Brotherhood without Banners on a murderous campaign against anyone connected to the Red Wedding.
prior_failed_quote: She don't speak. You bloody bastards cut her throat too deep for that. But she remembers.

paraphrase: House Karstark: Arnolf Karstark nominally joins Stannis but secretly plots to betray him to the Boltons, partly by forcibly marrying Karhold heiress Alys Karstark to his son Cregan to seize the lordship himself -- a real northern conspiracy, but a pro-Bolton one, not part of the pro-Stark plotting.
prior_failed_quote: Arnolf meant to use the girl to pry loose Karhold from her brother's hands. Having thus rid himself of Rickard Karstark and his uncle both, Arnolf would control both castles until Alys came of age.
prior_note: Arnolf's conspiracy to seize Karhold via forced marriage to Alys Karstark confirmed

paraphrase: Brandon was described as 'hot-blooded' and rash, a 'gallant fool' — a personality that fits someone who would defend Howland's honor by jousting in disguise.
prior_failed_quote: "Brandon," she said sadly. "Brandon was wild, and he was proud. He did not like to hear no for an answer.
prior_note: Catelyn describes Brandon as wild and proud—matches the paraphrase of 'hot-blooded' and 'gallant fool' personality

paraphrase: Brandon was 'tall,' which conflicts with the Knight's 'short of stature' description, a problem for identifying him as the mystery knight.
prior_failed_quote: He remembered Brandon's laughter, and Robert's berserk valor in the melee, the way he laughed as he unhorsed men left and right. He remembered Jaime Lannister, a golden youth in scaled white armor, kneeling on the grass in front of the king's pavilion and making his vows to protect and defend King Aerys. Afterward, Ser Oswell Whent helped Jaime to his feet, and the White Bull himself, Lord Commander Ser Gerold Hightower, fastened the snowy cloak of the Kingsguard about his shoulders.
prior_note: Reference to Brandon at Harrenhal tourney where he is not described as particularly short; contradicts 'short of stature' knight description

paraphrase: It was loyalty to Lannister, not a conspiracy against Targaryens, that led Pycelle to bring about the Mad King's downfall.
prior_failed_quote: All I did, I did for House Lannister. Always . . . for years . . . your lord father, ask him, I was ever his true servant
prior_note: Pycelle's primary motivation was Lannister loyalty, not maester conspiracy against Targaryens.
